results.name: the manifest κ pins are REAL — the on-disk 4K asset bytes re-derive to their declared sha256 (Law L5, independent oracle)
every chunk verified to its DECLARED κ before decode — verified all, refused none
bytes-rendered-before-verified = 0 (nothing decodes until its κ re-derives)
verified bytes persisted to the OpfsKappaStore (κ-store resident)
the 4K video actually DECODES — 3840×2160, frames advancing

ORIGIN KILL SWITCH — replays from the local OPFS κ-store, source flips to κ-store, zero origin (L3 + serverless)

spec: Holo Hub streaming showcase, Phase 1: the real 4K (2160p) Big Buck Bunny CMAF κ-object streams through the substrate — each chunk re-derives to its DECLARED manifest κ (Law L5) before it decodes (bytes-rendered-before-verify=0), persists in the OpfsKappaStore, decodes as real 4K video (where H.264 is present), and replays from the κ-store with the origin cut (L3 + serverless). Witnessed live in a real browser.
